title: 清洁女工之死
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\209-清洁女工之死-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot and Inspector Spence discuss the connection between recent deaths, identifying Evelyn Hope as a central figure. Poirot suggests that several suspects—Eva Kane, Lily Gambol, Janice Courtland, and Vera Black—may be linked to the Craig family, for whom Eva Kane previously served as a nanny. Poirot notes that Eva Kane was pregnant before leaving Britain following the execution of Craig, implying that her child may be a motive for revenge. To gather more information, Poirot makes a private call to the novelist Mrs. Ariadne Oliver regarding a small circus theater and individuals named Cecil and Michael. He subsequently informs Spence of his intention to visit Michael, a minor actor with the Karenquay Theater Company.

At the Long Grass Hotel, Poirot addresses a group of people to explain the pattern of the murders. Using a children's rhyme about the deaths of various women, such as Mrs. McQuitty and Mrs. MacKenzie, he illustrates the killings. He reveals that the investigation into the death of Mrs. McKittrick, for which James Bentley was wrongly convicted, was aided by a clue involving a bottle of ink and a photograph in *The Sunday Comet*. He explains that Mrs. McKittrick had recognized a photo of Eva Kane, who was connected to the Craig murder case. 

Poirot further reveals that Mrs. Upward attempted to mislead him by pointing to a photo of Lily Gambol when she had actually recognized a photo of Eva Kane. He notes that before her death, Mrs. Upward summoned three women—Eve Carpenter, Mrs. Rendell, and Miss Henderson—to see her privately. Poirot concludes that these three women share a commonality: they are approximately the same age as Eva Kane's daughter.
